name: Chicken Picardy with Dill Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 Leeks (separate the white and green parts)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Marcel Kerval of Cafe de France, St. Louis, MO
Preparation Time: 0:40
TO PREPARE CHICKEN PICARDY:
1. Pre-heat oven to 375°F.
2. Prepare eight squares of aluminum foil by cutting foil into sheets (6" x 6") and buttering them lightly on the shiny side.
3. Bone and skin chicken breasts. DO NOT HALVE. Flatten breasts between sheets of waxed paper with knife edge and place each breast on a square of foil.
4. Soak leeks in cold water, then drain well. Finely slice the green part of the leek, reserving 1/2 of it for each breast. Chop mushrooms, spinach leaves, and shallots.
5. In a skillet, sauté the chopped ingredients briefly (about 30 seconds) in butter over medium heat.
6. Divide the sautéed mixture evenly among the chicken breasts, placing about 1/4 of the mixture on each breast. Drizzle with white wine, salt, and pepper to taste.
7. Roll up each chicken breast carefully.
8. Fold the edges of the foil tightly to seal the parcels securely.
9. Place the wrapped chicken parcels in a baking pan filled with enough chicken stock to cover about 2-3 inches of the bottom.
10. Bake in the pre-heated oven for 25 minutes.
TO SERVE:
1. Unwrap the chicken breasts and slice into 1/4-inch rounds.
2. Serve the chicken over a portion of Dill Sauce.
3. Garnish with sautéed leek slices and serve immediately.
